label: world | be | beginning
sentence: According to a Haida story, in the beginning the world was in total darkness.

label: raven | be | ∅
sentence: The Raven, who had existed from the beginning of time, was tired of groping about and bumping into things in the dark.

label: raven | come | home
sentence: Eventually the Raven came upon the home of an old man who lived alone with his daughter.

label: raven | learn | slyness
sentence: Through his slyness, the Raven learned that the old man had a great treasure.

label: this | be | light
sentence: This was all the light in the universe, contained in a tiny box concealed within many boxes.

label: raven | vow | ∅
sentence: At once the Raven vowed to steal the light.

label: he | think | ∅
sentence: He thought and thought, and finally came up with a plan.

label: he | wait | ∅
sentence: He waited until the old man's daughter came to the river to gather water.

label: raven | change | himself
sentence: Then the Raven changed himself into a single hemlock needle and dropped himself into the river, just as the girl was dipping her water-basket into the river.

label: she | swallow | needle
sentence: As she drank from the basket, she swallowed the needle.

label: it | slip | ∅
sentence: It slipped and slithered down into her warm belly, where the Raven transformed himself again, this time into a tiny human.

label: raven | emerge | world
sentence: After sleeping and growing there for a very long time, at last the Raven emerged into the world once more, this time as a human infant.

label: grandfather | love | he
sentence: Even though he had a rather strange appearance, the Raven's grandfather loved him.

label: man | threaten | punishment
sentence: But the old man threatened dire punishment if he ever touched the precious treasure box.

label: ravenchild | beg | ∅
sentence: Nonetheless the Ravenchild begged and begged to be allowed to hold the light just for a moment.

label: man | yield | time
sentence: In time the old man yielded, and lifted from the box a warm and glowing sphere, which he threw to his grandson.

label: wing | spread | ∅
sentence: As the light was moving toward him, the human child transformed into a gigantic black shadowy bird-form, wings spread ready for flight, and beak open in anticipation.

label: raven | capture | it
sentence: As the beautiful ball of light reached him, the Raven captured it in his beak!

label: he | burst | smokehole
sentence: Moving his powerful wings, he burst through the smokehole in the roof of the house, and escaped into the darkness with his stolen treasure.

label: that | be | ∅
sentence: And that is how light came into the universe.
